page: Microsoft Azure DC1s_v2 is a single-core virtual machine belonging to the DCSv2 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon E-2288G processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 1 physical core, 1 thread, 4.0 GB of system memory, and 51 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. The CPU cache configuration consists of 32 KB L1d cache, 0.25 MB L2 cache, and 16 MB L3 cache.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but limited multi-threaded and memory throughput. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1895.19, and the Geekbench single-core score is 1278.0, both falling into the average category. However, because the instance is restricted to a single vCPU, multi-core benchmarks are poor, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 1332.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 2234.08. Memory bandwidth is also low, measuring 17.16 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 13.6 GB/sec for large block write operations.

From a resource density and cost perspective, this server represents a low-tier entry point designed for lightweight workloads that do not require parallel processing. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent single-thread execution, but the 4.0 GB memory capacity and low memory bandwidth limit its efficiency for data-intensive applications. Tradeoffs include poor database performance, as shown by a Passmark database score of 1052.38 and Redis SET operations at 1.77 million operations per second. This server is best suited for basic single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, utility scripting, and small-scale testing environments.
description: Microsoft Azure DC1s_v2 is a single-core virtual machine from the DCSv2 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon E-2288G processor operating at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 1 physical core, 1 thread, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 51 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage. Benchmark results show average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1278.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 1895.19. However, multi-core and memory performance are poor due to the single-vCPU limitation, with memory write bandwidth at 13.6 GB/sec and a Passmark database score of 1052.38. This entry-level configuration is qualitatively cost-effective for basic, non-parallel tasks but exhibits significant performance tradeoffs under multi-threaded workloads. It is suitable for lightweight web serving, single-threaded utility scripts, and basic development or testing environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC1s_v2 is a single-core virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon E-2288G processor, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 51 GB of local storage, designed for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

tagline: A dedicated single-core Intel processor instance with balanced memory for basic, non-parallel utility workloads and lightweight single-threaded applications.

4.0 GB of system memory and 51 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage

Suitable for lightweight web serving, utility scripting, and basic testing
